./specs/004-cpp-constexpr-upper-case/artifacts/specification/iter2-forge.md:12:- **US-2:** As a typist maintainer, I want the emitter code to produce `UPPER_SNAKE_CASE` constant names, so that golden tests reflect the canonical style and future regressions are caught.
./specs/004-cpp-constexpr-upper-case/artifacts/specification/iter2-forge.md:79:- **AC-1:** The C++ emitter (`src/typist/backends/cpp/emitter.py`) produces `UPPER_SNAKE_CASE` for all non-user-defined `constexpr` names as listed in FR-1, FR-2, and FR-3.
./specs/004-cpp-constexpr-upper-case/artifacts/specification/iter2-forge.md:80:- **AC-2:** The runtime emitter (`src/typist/backends/runtime/emitter.py`) produces `VERBOSE_DEFAULT` instead of `kVerboseDefault`.
./specs/004-cpp-constexpr-upper-case/artifacts/specification/iter2-forge.md:91:- Introducing Jinja2 templates for the C++ backend. **Exemption rationale:** This feature is a mechanical rename within existing string-built emitter functions and does not introduce new generated file structure or layout. Template migration is a separate initiative tracked independently.
./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/specification/iter4-forge.md:11:The Python, C++, and SystemVerilog backend emitters in `src/piketype/backends/{py,cpp,sv}/emitter.py` are currently single-file inline string-builders. Each file is 700–1100 lines (py: 792, cpp: 1067, sv: 949) and intermixes IR interpretation, formatting decisions, indentation, target-language syntax, helper-method bodies, and special cases. Auditing or extending any backend requires reading long Python functions whose output shape is implicit.
./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/specification/iter4-forge.md:26:- **US-1.** As a maintainer adding a new generated language construct, I want to edit a focused Jinja template alongside a small view-model addition, so that I can change output structure without re-reading 800-line emitter files.
./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/specification/iter4-forge.md:50:- **FR-2.** Each backend SHALL expose one Jinja2 `Environment` constructed via `jinja2.PackageLoader("piketype.backends.<lang>", "templates")`. Environment options SHALL be exactly: `keep_trailing_newline=True`, `trim_blocks=True`, `lstrip_blocks=True`, `undefined=jinja2.StrictUndefined`, `autoescape=False`. The environment SHALL be constructed once per `emit_<lang>` call (not module-global) so that emitter state is local and testable. `FileSystemLoader` SHALL NOT be used for production rendering. (Tests MAY construct ad-hoc environments with `DictLoader` or `FileSystemLoader` for unit testing of templates.)
./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/specification/iter4-forge.md:53:  - `def render(*, env: jinja2.Environment, template_name: str, context: object) -> str` that renders a template and returns a string whose final character is `"\n"` (consistent with current emitter contracts). The `context` SHALL be a view-model dataclass instance; the helper SHALL pass its fields as the template context (via `dataclasses.asdict` or by passing the instance directly with templates accessing `.field` attributes — implementation choice, but uniform across backends).
./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/specification/iter4-forge.md:54:- **FR-4.** Each backend SHALL keep its existing public emit entry point (`emit_py`, `emit_cpp`, `emit_sv`) with unchanged signatures and unchanged write locations; only the internal rendering pipeline changes. The IR → view-model → template → string flow SHALL be the only public migration surface.
./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/specification/iter4-forge.md:67:  **Examples of "trivial fragments" (MAY remain inline in Python and be passed as `str` fields):** a single hex literal (`"0xff"`), a numeric expression rendered for a constant (`"(1 << 32) - 1"`), a single-line type alias initializer, a header comment line, an `__all__` tuple body. These fragments SHALL be pre-computed in Python and exposed as `str` fields on the view model; templates SHALL emit them with `{{ fragment }}` and SHALL NOT recompute them.
./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/specification/iter4-forge.md:103:- **FR-13.** Template file extensions SHALL be `.j2`. Templates SHALL NOT carry the machine-readable header themselves; the header is produced by `backends/common/headers.py` and passed in as a view-model `header: str` field, then emitted by the template with `{{ header }}`.
./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/specification/iter4-forge.md:123:- **FR-19.** When a backend's migration is complete, the inline `render_module_<lang>` function and its `_render_*` helpers in `backends/<lang>/emitter.py` SHALL be removed. The `emit_<lang>` entry point remains and now orchestrates: (1) build view model from IR, (2) construct env, (3) render primary template, (4) write file.
./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/specification/iter4-forge.md:154:- **NFR-1. Performance.** The wall-clock time of `piketype gen` against fixture `tests/fixtures/struct_padded` SHALL NOT regress by more than 25% relative to the pre-migration baseline. The actual CLI signature is `piketype gen [--namespace NS] <dsl-file-path>`, where `<dsl-file-path>` is a single Python DSL file (e.g., `tests/fixtures/struct_padded/project/alpha/piketype/types.py`) — not a project root.
./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/specification/iter4-forge.md:172:- **NFR-7. Stability of public API.** The `emit_py`, `emit_cpp`, `emit_sv` function signatures and import paths SHALL NOT change. CLI behavior SHALL NOT change.
./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/specification/iter4-forge.md:185:- **AC-2-`<lang>`.** `find src/piketype/backends/<lang>/templates -name '*.j2' | wc -l` is at least 1 and `backends/<lang>/emitter.py` calls `render` from `backends/common/render.py` (verifiable by `grep -n "from piketype.backends.common.render import" src/piketype/backends/<lang>/emitter.py`).
./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/specification/iter4-forge.md:186:- **AC-3-`<lang>`.** `backends/<lang>/emitter.py` line count has decreased relative to its pre-migration count (commit `develop`@HEAD-at-spec-creation). Helpers `_render_*` whose responsibility moved to a template are removed (no dead code).
./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/specification/iter4-forge.md:210:- **OOS-2.** Migrating the runtime, build, lint, or test sub-backends under `backends/{runtime,build,lint,test}/`. Those are out of scope for this feature; only the three primary code emitters (py, cpp, sv) are in scope. Follow-up specs may address them.

./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/planning/iter3-forge.md:280:1. `feat(py): introduce ModuleView dataclasses and builders` — `view.py` complete, `tests/test_view_py.py` complete. `emitter.py` unchanged. View module is dormant; tests pass against it directly.
./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/planning/iter3-forge.md:281:2. `feat(py): module skeleton via templates` (FR-6 sub-step 1) — `module.j2` renders header + imports + footer. `emit_py` constructs `ModuleView` with `body_lines = tuple(legacy_render_body_lines(module))` and the template emits `{{ body_lines | join("\n") }}` for the body. **Byte parity preserved.**
./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/planning/iter3-forge.md:282:3. `feat(py): top-level type declaration skeletons via templates` (FR-6 sub-step 2) — `_macros.j2` adds `scalar_class_skeleton`, `struct_class_skeleton`, `enum_classes_skeleton`, `flags_class_skeleton` macros that emit *only the class header lines* (`class X:`, `WIDTH = ...`, `BYTE_COUNT = ...`). Helper-method bodies and expression fragments still arrive as `body_lines_per_type: tuple[str, ...]` strings on each TypeView. The view is augmented with `helper_lines: tuple[str, ...]` per TypeView temporarily. **Byte parity preserved.**
./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/planning/iter3-forge.md:283:4. `feat(py): helper-method skeletons via templates` (FR-6 sub-step 3) — macros gain `to_bytes_block`, `from_bytes_block`, `coerce_block`, `setattr_block`, `clone_block`, `eq_repr_block`. Each block consumes precomputed numeric primitives from the view and emits the helper body skeleton. The transitional `helper_lines` field is removed. **Byte parity preserved.**
./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/planning/iter3-forge.md:285:6. `refactor(py): remove inline emitter helpers` (FR-19) — delete every `_render_py_*` function and the legacy `render_module_py`. `emit_py` becomes ≤ ~40 lines. **Byte parity preserved.**

./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/planning/iter3-forge.md:632:| Performance regression > 25% from Jinja template loading overhead | Low–Medium | Measured at every per-backend completion (FR-25). `jinja2.Environment` caches compiled templates by default within a process. `emit_<lang>` constructs the env once per call, so all modules in a single `gen` invocation share compiled-template caches. Mitigation if exceeded: switch to `jinja2.PackageLoader` with bytecode caching enabled. |
./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/planning/iter3-forge.md:634:| C++ `--namespace` CLI override regression | Medium | `tests/test_view_cpp.py` runs the namespace-override fixture (`tests/fixtures/namespace_override`) end-to-end, asserting the rendered guard macro and namespace open/close strings. ./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/planning/iter2-forge.md:35:- `emit_<lang>` signature unchanged (FR-4, NFR-7).
./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/planning/iter2-forge.md:41:Each Phase 1/2/3 sub-step commit replaces one slice of the inline emitter with a template, while remaining slices continue to render via the legacy inline path. The plan keeps byte parity at every commit by passing the not-yet-migrated slices through to the template as **already-rendered strings**.
./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/planning/iter2-forge.md:44:- `ModuleView` carries a field `body_lines: tuple[str, ...] | None`. When non-None, the primary template emits `{{ body_lines | join("\n") }}` for the not-yet-templated portion.
./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/planning/iter2-forge.md:48:This is internal scaffolding strictly contained inside view + template; it does not leak to the public emit API and does not survive into the post-cleanup state.
./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/planning/iter2-forge.md:54:**Goal:** Land everything that must exist before the first backend migration. After this phase, the repository builds, all existing tests pass, and the new tools are runnable. No emitter changes yet.
./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/planning/iter2-forge.md:60:| `src/piketype/backends/common/render.py`                        | rewrite | `make_environment(*, package: str) -> jinja2.Environment` and `render(*, env: jinja2.Environment, template_name: str, context: object) -> str`. Custom-filter registration site (CL-2/FR-16). |

./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/planning/iter2-forge.md:106:    output = env.get_template(template_name).render(**ctx)
./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/planning/iter2-forge.md:154:| `src/piketype/backends/py/emitter.py`             | rewrite | Shrink to: build view → render → write. Remove `_render_*` helpers (FR-19). |
./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/planning/iter2-forge.md:276:1. `feat(py): introduce ModuleView dataclasses and builders` — `view.py` complete, `tests/test_view_py.py` complete. `emitter.py` unchanged. View module is dormant; tests pass against it directly.
./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/planning/iter2-forge.md:277:2. `feat(py): module skeleton via templates` (FR-6 sub-step 1) — `module.j2` renders header + imports + footer. `emit_py` constructs `ModuleView` with `body_lines = tuple(legacy_render_body_lines(module))` and the template emits `{{ body_lines | join("\n") }}` for the body. **Byte parity preserved.**
./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/planning/iter2-forge.md:278:3. `feat(py): top-level type declaration skeletons via templates` (FR-6 sub-step 2) — `_macros.j2` adds `scalar_class_skeleton`, `struct_class_skeleton`, `enum_classes_skeleton`, `flags_class_skeleton` macros that emit *only the class header lines* (`class X:`, `WIDTH = ...`, `BYTE_COUNT = ...`). Helper-method bodies and expression fragments still arrive as `body_lines_per_type: tuple[str, ...]` strings on each TypeView. The view is augmented with `helper_lines: tuple[str, ...]` per TypeView temporarily. **Byte parity preserved.**
./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/planning/iter2-forge.md:279:4. `feat(py): helper-method skeletons via templates` (FR-6 sub-step 3) — macros gain `to_bytes_block`, `from_bytes_block`, `coerce_block`, `setattr_block`, `clone_block`, `eq_repr_block`. Each block consumes precomputed numeric primitives from the view and emits the helper body skeleton. The transitional `helper_lines` field is removed. **Byte parity preserved.**
./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/planning/iter2-forge.md:281:6. `refactor(py): remove inline emitter helpers` (FR-19) — delete every `_render_py_*` function and the legacy `render_module_py`. `emit_py` becomes ≤ ~40 lines. **Byte parity preserved.**
./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/planning/iter2-forge.md:298:class CppNamespaceView:
./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/planning/iter2-forge.md:299:    open_lines: tuple[str, ...]   # ["namespace alpha {", "namespace piketype {"]
./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/planning/iter2-forge.md:300:    close_lines: tuple[str, ...]  # ["}  // namespace piketype", "}  // namespace alpha"]
./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/planning/iter2-forge.md:334:    namespace: CppNamespaceView
./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/planning/iter2-forge.md:339:    standard_includes: tuple[str, ...]   # sorted "<cstdint>", "<cstddef>", "<stdexcept>", "<vector>"
./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/planning/iter2-forge.md:349:**`--namespace` CLI override:** the namespace builder respects `emit_cpp(repo, *, namespace=None)`. The view's `CppNamespaceView` is constructed from either `module.ref.namespace_parts` (default) or the CLI override (test in `tests/test_view_cpp.py` includes a fixture run with an override).
./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/planning/iter2-forge.md:354:2. `feat(cpp): module skeleton via templates` (header, guard open, includes, namespace open, namespace close, guard close).
./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/planning/iter2-forge.md:358:6. `refactor(cpp): remove inline emitter helpers`.
./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/planning/iter2-forge.md:454:6. `refactor(sv): remove inline emitter helpers`.
./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/planning/iter2-forge.md:489:| Performance regression > 25% from Jinja template loading overhead | Low–Medium | Measured at every per-backend completion (FR-25). `jinja2.Environment` caches compiled templates by default within a process. `emit_<lang>` constructs the env once per call, so all modules in a single `gen` invocation share compiled-template caches. Mitigation if exceeded: switch to `jinja2.PackageLoader` with bytecode caching enabled. |
./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/planning/iter2-forge.md:491:| C++ `--namespace` CLI override regression | Medium | `tests/test_view_cpp.py` runs the namespace-override fixture (`tests/fixtures/namespace_override`) end-to-end, asserting the rendered guard macro and namespace open/close strings. This catches regressions before goldens. |

./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/specification/iter4-gauge.md:62:The Python, C++, and SystemVerilog backend emitters in `src/piketype/backends/{py,cpp,sv}/emitter.py` are currently single-file inline string-builders. Each file is 700–1100 lines (py: 792, cpp: 1067, sv: 949) and intermixes IR interpretation, formatting decisions, indentation, target-language syntax, helper-method bodies, and special cases. Auditing or extending any backend requires reading long Python functions whose output shape is implicit.
./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/specification/iter4-gauge.md:77:- **US-1.** As a maintainer adding a new generated language construct, I want to edit a focused Jinja template alongside a small view-model addition, so that I can change output structure without re-reading 800-line emitter files.
./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/specification/iter4-gauge.md:101:- **FR-2.** Each backend SHALL expose one Jinja2 `Environment` constructed via `jinja2.PackageLoader("piketype.backends.<lang>", "templates")`. Environment options SHALL be exactly: `keep_trailing_newline=True`, `trim_blocks=True`, `lstrip_blocks=True`, `undefined=jinja2.StrictUndefined`, `autoescape=False`. The environment SHALL be constructed once per `emit_<lang>` call (not module-global) so that emitter state is local and testable. `FileSystemLoader` SHALL NOT be used for production rendering. (Tests MAY construct ad-hoc environments with `DictLoader` or `FileSystemLoader` for unit testing of templates.)
./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/specification/iter4-gauge.md:104:  - `def render(*, env: jinja2.Environment, template_name: str, context: object) -> str` that renders a template and returns a string whose final character is `"\n"` (consistent with current emitter contracts). The `context` SHALL be a view-model dataclass instance; the helper SHALL pass its fields as the template context (via `dataclasses.asdict` or by passing the instance directly with templates accessing `.field` attributes — implementation choice, but uniform across backends).
./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/specification/iter4-gauge.md:105:- **FR-4.** Each backend SHALL keep its existing public emit entry point (`emit_py`, `emit_cpp`, `emit_sv`) with unchanged signatures and unchanged write locations; only the internal rendering pipeline changes. The IR → view-model → template → string flow SHALL be the only public migration surface.
./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/specification/iter4-gauge.md:118:  **Examples of "trivial fragments" (MAY remain inline in Python and be passed as `str` fields):** a single hex literal (`"0xff"`), a numeric expression rendered for a constant (`"(1 << 32) - 1"`), a single-line type alias initializer, a header comment line, an `__all__` tuple body. These fragments SHALL be pre-computed in Python and exposed as `str` fields on the view model; templates SHALL emit them with `{{ fragment }}` and SHALL NOT recompute them.
./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/specification/iter4-gauge.md:154:- **FR-13.** Template file extensions SHALL be `.j2`. Templates SHALL NOT carry the machine-readable header themselves; the header is produced by `backends/common/headers.py` and passed in as a view-model `header: str` field, then emitted by the template with `{{ header }}`.
./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/specification/iter4-gauge.md:174:- **FR-19.** When a backend's migration is complete, the inline `render_module_<lang>` function and its `_render_*` helpers in `backends/<lang>/emitter.py` SHALL be removed. The `emit_<lang>` entry point remains and now orchestrates: (1) build view model from IR, (2) construct env, (3) render primary template, (4) write file.
./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/specification/iter4-gauge.md:205:- **NFR-1. Performance.** The wall-clock time of `piketype gen` against fixture `tests/fixtures/struct_padded` SHALL NOT regress by more than 25% relative to the pre-migration baseline. The actual CLI signature is `piketype gen [--namespace NS] <dsl-file-path>`, where `<dsl-file-path>` is a single Python DSL file (e.g., `tests/fixtures/struct_padded/project/alpha/piketype/types.py`) — not a project root.
./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/specification/iter4-gauge.md:223:- **NFR-7. Stability of public API.** The `emit_py`, `emit_cpp`, `emit_sv` function signatures and import paths SHALL NOT change. CLI behavior SHALL NOT change.
./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/specification/iter4-gauge.md:236:- **AC-2-`<lang>`.** `find src/piketype/backends/<lang>/templates -name '*.j2' | wc -l` is at least 1 and `backends/<lang>/emitter.py` calls `render` from `backends/common/render.py` (verifiable by `grep -n "from piketype.backends.common.render import" src/piketype/backends/<lang>/emitter.py`).
./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/specification/iter4-gauge.md:237:- **AC-3-`<lang>`.** `backends/<lang>/emitter.py` line count has decreased relative to its pre-migration count (commit `develop`@HEAD-at-spec-creation). Helpers `_render_*` whose responsibility moved to a template are removed (no dead code).
./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/specification/iter4-gauge.md:261:- **OOS-2.** Migrating the runtime, build, lint, or test sub-backends under `backends/{runtime,build,lint,test}/`. Those are out of scope for this feature; only the three primary code emitters (py, cpp, sv) are in scope. Follow-up specs may address them.
./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/specification/iter4-gauge.md:305:1. BLOCKING — NFR-1/AC-F4/AC-F5: spec referenced a nonexistent `gen_main` and an unsupported `--repo` flag. The actual CLI is `piketype gen [--namespace NS] <dsl-file>`; the programmatic entry is `piketype.commands.gen.run_gen(path: str, *, namespace: str | None = None)`.
./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/specification/iter4-gauge.md:480:  backends/          -- code emitters (sv/, py/, cpp/, runtime/, build/, test/, lint/)

./specs/002-namespace-cli-argument/artifacts/specification/iter3-gauge.md:12:  namespace values that pass FR-2 can still generate reserved macro identifiers.

./specs/002-namespace-cli-argument/artifacts/specification/iter4-gauge.md:8:- **Suggestion:** Either validate only the namespace-derived guard prefix before discovery, or make full per-module include-guard validation in scope.
./specs/002-namespace-cli-argument/artifacts/specification/iter4-gauge.md:13:- **Suggestion:** Add a negative test for `typist gen --namespace "foo::_bar" <path>`.
./specs/004-cpp-constexpr-upper-case/artifacts/specification/iter1-gauge.md:7:- **Issue:** The overview says every generated C++ `constexpr` must use `UPPER_SNAKE_CASE`, and the out-of-scope list only excludes variables that are not `constexpr`. That is not what the requirements or acceptance criteria enforce. The C++ emitter also generates local `constexpr std::uint64_t mask` variables in inline scalar encode/decode helpers (`src/typist/backends/cpp/emitter.py:599`, `:637`, `:648`), and the current goldens contain those lowercase `constexpr` names (`tests/goldens/gen/struct_signed/cpp/alpha/typist/types_types.hpp:200`, `:217`). FR-3 only covers `kMinValue` and `kMaxValue` inside `validate_<field>`, and AC-6 only rejects `kCamelCase` constexpr names. An implementation can leave `mask` lowercase, pass AC-1 and AC-6 as written, and still violate the overview.
./specs/004-cpp-constexpr-upper-case/artifacts/specification/iter1-gauge.md:12:- **Issue:** FR-1 says references must be updated in method bodies such as `to_bytes`, `from_bytes`, and `validate_value`, but scalar alias generation also uses `kByteCount` in the wide-scalar default constructor initializer (`src/typist/backends/cpp/emitter.py:303`) and in `validate_value` (`:322`). The named method list is too narrow. If an implementer follows the text literally, generated C++ can still reference removed `k...` symbols after declarations are renamed.
./specs/004-cpp-constexpr-upper-case/artifacts/specification/iter1-gauge.md:22:- **Issue:** The spec puts Jinja2 template work out of scope. The constitution says new or changed generated outputs should be backed by templates wherever practical (`.steel/constitution.md:47-51`). This rename may be small enough to exempt, but the spec does not say why direct emitter string edits are acceptable here.
./specs/004-cpp-constexpr-upper-case/artifacts/specification/iter1-gauge.md:23:- **Suggestion:** Add a short explicit exemption: this feature is a mechanical rename in existing string-built emitters and does not introduce new generated structure, so template migration is deferred.
./specs/002-namespace-cli-argument/artifacts/specification/iter4-forge.md:1:# Spec 002: `--namespace` CLI Argument for C++ Namespace Override
./specs/002-namespace-cli-argument/artifacts/specification/iter4-forge.md:5:Add a `--namespace` CLI argument to `typist gen` that lets users specify a custom
./specs/002-namespace-cli-argument/artifacts/specification/iter4-forge.md:6:top-level C++ namespace prefix. Today, the C++ namespace is derived entirely from
./specs/002-namespace-cli-argument/artifacts/specification/iter4-forge.md:8:out). This feature gives users explicit control over the generated C++ namespace
./specs/002-namespace-cli-argument/artifacts/specification/iter4-forge.md:14:typist gen --namespace foo::bar constants.py
./specs/002-namespace-cli-argument/artifacts/specification/iter4-forge.md:20:namespace foo::bar::constants {
./specs/002-namespace-cli-argument/artifacts/specification/iter4-forge.md:22:}  // namespace foo::bar::constants
./specs/002-namespace-cli-argument/artifacts/specification/iter4-forge.md:25:Instead of the current path-derived namespace (e.g., `alpha::constants`).
./specs/002-namespace-cli-argument/artifacts/specification/iter4-forge.md:29:1. **As a hardware/firmware engineer**, I want to specify the C++ namespace for
./specs/002-namespace-cli-argument/artifacts/specification/iter4-forge.md:31:   namespace hierarchy, without being forced to mirror the DSL file-system layout.
./specs/002-namespace-cli-argument/artifacts/specification/iter4-forge.md:34:   namespace so that CI scripts can target different namespace schemes per build
./specs/002-namespace-cli-argument/artifacts/specification/iter4-forge.md:39:### FR-1: New `--namespace` argument on the `gen` subcommand
./specs/002-namespace-cli-argument/artifacts/specification/iter4-forge.md:41:The `gen` subcommand accepts an optional `--namespace <value>` argument.
./specs/002-namespace-cli-argument/artifacts/specification/iter4-forge.md:43:- `<value>` is a `::` -separated C++ namespace path (e.g., `foo`, `foo::bar`,
./specs/002-namespace-cli-argument/artifacts/specification/iter4-forge.md:46:  (path-derived namespace with `typist` segment filtered).
./specs/002-namespace-cli-argument/artifacts/specification/iter4-forge.md:61:   words (e.g., `class`, `namespace`, `return`, `int`, `void`, etc.) and rejects
./specs/002-namespace-cli-argument/artifacts/specification/iter4-forge.md:83:### FR-3: C++ namespace composition
./specs/002-namespace-cli-argument/artifacts/specification/iter4-forge.md:85:When `--namespace` is provided, the generated C++ namespace for a module is:
./specs/002-namespace-cli-argument/artifacts/specification/iter4-forge.md:88:<namespace_value>::<module_basename>
./specs/002-namespace-cli-argument/artifacts/specification/iter4-forge.md:93:parts is bypassed entirely — the user-provided namespace replaces it.
./specs/002-namespace-cli-argument/artifacts/specification/iter4-forge.md:95:When `--namespace` is **not** provided, the existing path-derived behaviour is
./specs/002-namespace-cli-argument/artifacts/specification/iter4-forge.md:100:When `--namespace` is provided, the include guard follows the same composition:
./specs/002-namespace-cli-argument/artifacts/specification/iter4-forge.md:109:When omitted, the existing guard derivation from `namespace_parts` is unchanged.
./specs/002-namespace-cli-argument/artifacts/specification/iter4-forge.md:113:The `--namespace` argument affects **only** generated C++ module headers (the
./specs/002-namespace-cli-argument/artifacts/specification/iter4-forge.md:118:- Manifest `namespace_parts`
./specs/002-namespace-cli-argument/artifacts/specification/iter4-forge.md:121:  keep their existing `typist_runtime` namespace and include guards unchanged.
./specs/002-namespace-cli-argument/artifacts/specification/iter4-forge.md:124:are file-path-derived. Runtime headers have a fixed, well-known namespace.
./specs/002-namespace-cli-argument/artifacts/specification/iter4-forge.md:125:A C++ namespace override should not silently alter any of these.
./specs/002-namespace-cli-argument/artifacts/specification/iter4-forge.md:129:The namespace override value is carried from CLI → `run_gen` → C++ emitter. It
./specs/002-namespace-cli-argument/artifacts/specification/iter4-forge.md:130:does **not** modify `ModuleRefIR.namespace_parts` in the IR. Instead, it is
./specs/002-namespace-cli-argument/artifacts/specification/iter4-forge.md:136:When `--namespace` is specified, it applies to **every** module discovered in the
./specs/002-namespace-cli-argument/artifacts/specification/iter4-forge.md:137:generation run, not just the CLI-targeted module. All emitted C++ module headers
./specs/002-namespace-cli-argument/artifacts/specification/iter4-forge.md:138:in the run use the same namespace prefix.
./specs/002-namespace-cli-argument/artifacts/specification/iter4-forge.md:141:in the repo. Applying the namespace only to the targeted module would create an
./specs/002-namespace-cli-argument/artifacts/specification/iter4-forge.md:146:When `--namespace` is specified, the tool validates that no two discovered
./specs/002-namespace-cli-argument/artifacts/specification/iter4-forge.md:151:Rationale: With `--namespace`, the C++ namespace is `<prefix>::<basename>`. If
./specs/002-namespace-cli-argument/artifacts/specification/iter4-forge.md:153:`beta/typist/types.py`), they would produce identical namespaces
./specs/002-namespace-cli-argument/artifacts/specification/iter4-forge.md:162:`--namespace` value, consistent with the project constitution.
./specs/002-namespace-cli-argument/artifacts/specification/iter4-forge.md:171:When `--namespace` is omitted, all generated outputs are byte-for-byte identical

./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/specification/iter1-gauge.md:147:  backends/          -- code emitters (sv/, py/, cpp/, runtime/, build/, test/, lint/)
./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/specification/iter1-gauge.md:162:  - Scope matches the subsystem: `sv`, `cpp`, `py`, `dsl`, `emitter`, `codegen`, `init`, etc.
./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/specification/iter1-gauge.md:181:5. Add emission in each backend (`sv/emitter.py`, `cpp/emitter.py`, `py/emitter.py`).
./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/specification/iter1-gauge.md:208:The Python, C++, and SystemVerilog backend emitters in `src/piketype/backends/{py,cpp,sv}/emitter.py` are currently single-file inline string-builders. Each file is 700–1100 lines (py: 792, cpp: 1067, sv: 949) and intermixes IR interpretation, formatting decisions, indentation, target-language syntax, helper-method bodies, and special cases. Auditing or extending any backend requires reading long Python functions whose output shape is implicit.
./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/specification/iter1-gauge.md:223:- **US-1.** As a maintainer adding a new generated language construct, I want to edit a focused Jinja template alongside a small view-model addition, so that I can change output structure without re-reading 800-line emitter files.
./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/specification/iter1-gauge.md:237:- **FR-2.** Each backend SHALL expose one Jinja2 environment scoped to its `templates/` directory using a `FileSystemLoader` rooted at `backends/<lang>/templates/`. Environment options SHALL include `keep_trailing_newline=True`, `trim_blocks=True`, `lstrip_blocks=True`, `undefined=jinja2.StrictUndefined`, and `autoescape=False`. Environments SHALL be constructed once per `emit_<lang>` call (not module-global) to keep emitter state local and testable.
./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/specification/iter1-gauge.md:240:  - A helper to render a template and return a `str` whose final character is `"\n"` (consistent with current emitter contracts).
./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/specification/iter1-gauge.md:241:- **FR-4.** Each backend SHALL keep its existing public emit entry point (`emit_py`, `emit_cpp`, `emit_sv`) with unchanged signatures and unchanged write locations; only the internal rendering pipeline changes. The IR → view-model → template → string flow SHALL be the only public migration surface.
./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/specification/iter1-gauge.md:245:- **FR-5.** Migration SHALL proceed in this order: (1) Python backend, (2) C++ backend, (3) SystemVerilog backend. Each backend migration SHALL be a separable, independently reviewable change. Subsequent backends SHALL NOT begin until the prior backend's golden tests pass byte-for-byte and its inline emitter has been removed.
./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/specification/iter1-gauge.md:292:- **FR-19.** When a backend's migration is complete, the inline `render_module_<lang>` function and its helpers in `backends/<lang>/emitter.py` SHALL be removed (no dead code retained "just in case"). The `emit_<lang>` entry point remains.
./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/specification/iter1-gauge.md:314:- **NFR-7. Stability of public API.** The `emit_py`, `emit_cpp`, `emit_sv` function signatures and import paths SHALL NOT change. CLI behavior SHALL NOT change.
./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/specification/iter1-gauge.md:321:- **AC-2.** `find src/piketype/backends/{py,cpp,sv}/templates -name '*.j2'` lists at least one template per backend, and each backend's `emit_<lang>` calls into a Jinja2 `Environment` to produce its output.
./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/specification/iter1-gauge.md:322:- **AC-3.** Each backend's `emitter.py` has shrunk relative to its pre-migration line count. Helper functions whose responsibility is now in templates have been removed (no dead code).
./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/specification/iter1-gauge.md:335:- **OOS-2.** Migrating the runtime, build, lint, or test sub-backends under `backends/{runtime,build,lint,test}/`. Those are out of scope for v1 of this feature; only the three primary code emitters (py, cpp, sv) are in scope.
./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/specification/iter1-gauge.md:350:- **[NEEDS CLARIFICATION Q-5]** How should the migration treat `backends/runtime/emitter.py` (the runtime support package generator)? It is currently the only emitter that already has imports of "Template" related symbols (per the earlier grep). Is it in scope as a fourth migration, deferred to a follow-up spec, or considered out of scope per OOS-2? **Recommendation:** explicitly out of scope here (per OOS-2); follow-up spec after the three primary backends land. Confirm.

./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/specification/iter1-gauge.md:1378:The Python, C++, and SystemVerilog backend emitters in `src/piketype/backends/{py,cpp,sv}/emitter.py` are currently single-file inline string-builders. Each file is 700–1100 lines (py: 792, cpp: 1067, sv: 949) and intermixes IR interpretation, formatting decisions, indentation, target-language syntax, helper-method bodies, and special cases. Auditing or extending any backend requires reading long Python functions whose output shape is implicit.
./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/specification/iter1-gauge.md:1393:- **US-1.** As a maintainer adding a new generated language construct, I want to edit a focused Jinja template alongside a small view-model addition, so that I can change output structure without re-reading 800-line emitter files.
./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/specification/iter1-gauge.md:1407:- **FR-2.** Each backend SHALL expose one Jinja2 environment scoped to its `templates/` directory using a `FileSystemLoader` rooted at `backends/<lang>/templates/`. Environment options SHALL include `keep_trailing_newline=True`, `trim_blocks=True`, `lstrip_blocks=True`, `undefined=jinja2.StrictUndefined`, and `autoescape=False`. Environments SHALL be constructed once per `emit_<lang>` call (not module-global) to keep emitter state local and testable.
./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/specification/iter1-gauge.md:1410:  - A helper to render a template and return a `str` whose final character is `"\n"` (consistent with current emitter contracts).

./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/planning/iter2-gauge.md:116:Each Phase 1/2/3 sub-step commit replaces one slice of the inline emitter with a template, while remaining slices continue to render via the legacy inline path. The plan keeps byte parity at every commit by passing the not-yet-migrated slices through to the template as **already-rendered strings**.
./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/planning/iter2-gauge.md:119:- `ModuleView` carries a field `body_lines: tuple[str, ...] | None`. When non-None, the primary template emits `{{ body_lines | join("\n") }}` for the not-yet-templated portion.
./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/planning/iter2-gauge.md:123:This is internal scaffolding strictly contained inside view + template; it does not leak to the public emit API and does not survive into the post-cleanup state.
./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/planning/iter2-gauge.md:129:**Goal:** Land everything that must exist before the first backend migration. After this phase, the repository builds, all existing tests pass, and the new tools are runnable. No emitter changes yet.

./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/planning/iter2-gauge.md:416:The Python, C++, and SystemVerilog backend emitters in `src/piketype/backends/{py,cpp,sv}/emitter.py` are currently single-file inline string-builders. Each file is 700–1100 lines (py: 792, cpp: 1067, sv: 949) and intermixes IR interpretation, formatting decisions, indentation, target-language syntax, helper-method bodies, and special cases. Auditing or extending any backend requires reading long Python functions whose output shape is implicit.
./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/planning/iter2-gauge.md:431:- **US-1.** As a maintainer adding a new generated language construct, I want to edit a focused Jinja template alongside a small view-model addition, so that I can change output structure without re-reading 800-line emitter files.
./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/planning/iter2-gauge.md:455:- **FR-2.** Each backend SHALL expose one Jinja2 `Environment` constructed via `jinja2.PackageLoader("piketype.backends.<lang>", "templates")`. Environment options SHALL be exactly: `keep_trailing_newline=True`, `trim_blocks=True`, `lstrip_blocks=True`, `undefined=jinja2.StrictUndefined`, `autoescape=False`. The environment SHALL be constructed once per `emit_<lang>` call (not module-global) so that emitter state is local and testable. `FileSystemLoader` SHALL NOT be used for production rendering. (Tests MAY construct ad-hoc environments with `DictLoader` or `FileSystemLoader` for unit testing of templates.)
./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/planning/iter2-gauge.md:458:  - `def render(*, env: jinja2.Environment, template_name: str, context: object) -> str` that renders a template and returns a string whose final character is `"\n"` (consistent with current emitter contracts). The `context` SHALL be a view-model dataclass instance; the helper SHALL pass its fields as the template context (via `dataclasses.asdict` or by passing the instance directly with templates accessing `.field` attributes — implementation choice, but uniform across backends).
./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/planning/iter2-gauge.md:459:- **FR-4.** Each backend SHALL keep its existing public emit entry point (`emit_py`, `emit_cpp`, `emit_sv`) with unchanged signatures and unchanged write locations; only the internal rendering pipeline changes. The IR → view-model → template → string flow SHALL be the only public migration surface.
./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/planning/iter2-gauge.md:472:  **Examples of "trivial fragments" (MAY remain inline in Python and be passed as `str` fields):** a single hex literal (`"0xff"`), a numeric expression rendered for a constant (`"(1 << 32) - 1"`), a single-line type alias initializer, a header comment line, an `__all__` tuple body. These fragments SHALL be pre-computed in Python and exposed as `str` fields on the view model; templates SHALL emit them with `{{ fragment }}` and SHALL NOT recompute them.
./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/planning/iter2-gauge.md:508:- **FR-13.** Template file extensions SHALL be `.j2`. Templates SHALL NOT carry the machine-readable header themselves; the header is produced by `backends/common/headers.py` and passed in as a view-model `header: str` field, then emitted by the template with `{{ header }}`.
./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/planning/iter2-gauge.md:528:- **FR-19.** When a backend's migration is complete, the inline `render_module_<lang>` function and its `_render_*` helpers in `backends/<lang>/emitter.py` SHALL be removed. The `emit_<lang>` entry point remains and now orchestrates: (1) build view model from IR, (2) construct env, (3) render primary template, (4) write file.
./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/planning/iter2-gauge.md:559:- **NFR-1. Performance.** The wall-clock time of `piketype gen` against fixture `tests/fixtures/struct_padded` SHALL NOT regress by more than 25% relative to the pre-migration baseline. The actual CLI signature is `piketype gen [--namespace NS] <dsl-file-path>`, where `<dsl-file-path>` is a single Python DSL file (e.g., `tests/fixtures/struct_padded/project/alpha/piketype/types.py`) — not a project root.
./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/planning/iter2-gauge.md:577:- **NFR-7. Stability of public API.** The `emit_py`, `emit_cpp`, `emit_sv` function signatures and import paths SHALL NOT change. CLI behavior SHALL NOT change.
./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/planning/iter2-gauge.md:590:- **AC-2-`<lang>`.** `find src/piketype/backends/<lang>/templates -name '*.j2' | wc -l` is at least 1 and `backends/<lang>/emitter.py` calls `render` from `backends/common/render.py` (verifiable by `grep -n "from piketype.backends.common.render import" src/piketype/backends/<lang>/emitter.py`).
./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/planning/iter2-gauge.md:591:- **AC-3-`<lang>`.** `backends/<lang>/emitter.py` line count has decreased relative to its pre-migration count (commit `develop`@HEAD-at-spec-creation). Helpers `_render_*` whose responsibility moved to a template are removed (no dead code).
./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/planning/iter2-gauge.md:615:- **OOS-2.** Migrating the runtime, build, lint, or test sub-backends under `backends/{runtime,build,lint,test}/`. Those are out of scope for this feature; only the three primary code emitters (py, cpp, sv) are in scope. Follow-up specs may address them.
./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/planning/iter2-gauge.md:628:- **CL-1 (was Q-1) — Per-backend commit granularity.** Each backend's migration lands as **multiple contiguous commits** on `feature/010-jinja-template-migration`, one per FR-6 sub-step where the change is non-trivial (skeleton, top-level types, helpers, fragments). The first commit of a backend's migration block introduces `view.py` and the primary template; subsequent commits replace inline emitter sections with template-rendered output. Squash-on-merge is permitted by project policy but SHALL NOT be required by this spec. Reverting any backend's migration is a contiguous-range revert per NFR-6.
./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/planning/iter2-gauge.md:689:4. **Repository state:** the `pike-type` project root. You may inspect files to validate concrete claims (CLI signatures, current emitter sizes, fixture paths).

./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/planning/iter2-gauge.md:703:   - Does C++/SV need any additional view fields the plan does not mention? (e.g., C++ namespace nesting strings, include guard symbols, SV `_pkg`/`_test_pkg` distinction.)
./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/planning/iter2-gauge.md:713:   - C++ `namespace` rendering depending on `--namespace` CLI override.

Custom-filter registration site (CL-2/FR-16). |
./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/planning/iter2-gauge.md:1234:./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/plan.md:277:2. `feat(py): module skeleton via templates` (FR-6 sub-step 1) — `module.j2` renders header + imports + footer. `emit_py` constructs `ModuleView` with `body_lines = tuple(legacy_render_body_lines(module))` and the template emits `{{ body_lines | join("\n") }}` for the body. **Byte parity preserved.**
./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/planning/iter2-gauge.md:1235:./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/plan.md:278:3. `feat(py): top-level type declaration skeletons via templates` (FR-6 sub-step 2) — `_macros.j2` adds `scalar_class_skeleton`, `struct_class_skeleton`, `enum_classes_skeleton`, `flags_class_skeleton` macros that emit *only the class header lines* (`class X:`, `WIDTH = ...`, `BYTE_COUNT = ...`). Helper-method bodies and expression fragments still arrive as `body_lines_per_type: tuple[str, ...]` strings on each TypeView. The view is augmented with `helper_lines: tuple[str, ...]` per TypeView temporarily. **Byte parity preserved.**

./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/planning/iter2-gauge.md:1483:./specs/002-namespace-cli-argument/artifacts/specification/iter2-gauge.md:7:- **Issue:** FR-2 still permits reserved C++ identifiers. C++ reserves identifiers containing `__` anywhere, not only with a `__` prefix. It also reserves names beginning with `_` in the global namespace, so `--namespace _foo` is unsafe. The spec also allows `--namespace std::x`, which generates declarations inside `std`, which is not valid user code.
./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/planning/iter2-gauge.md:1484:./specs/002-namespace-cli-argument/artifacts/specification/iter2-gauge.md:12:- **Issue:** FR-7 says "All emitted C++ headers in the run use the same namespace prefix," but the project also emits `cpp/runtime/typist_runtime.hpp`. AC-1 narrows this to module namespaces, so the runtime header behavior is unclear.
./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/planning/iter2-gauge.md:1485:./specs/002-namespace-cli-argument/artifacts/specification/iter2-gauge.md:13:- **Suggestion:** State explicitly that `--namespace` affects generated module C++ headers only, and that runtime C++ headers keep their existing namespace and include guard. If runtime should change too, add explicit requirements and acceptance criteria.
./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/planning/iter2-gauge.md:1486:./specs/002-namespace-cli-argument/artifacts/specification/iter2-gauge.md:18:- **Suggestion:** Require the positive golden-file case to include at least two discovered modules with different path-derived namespaces, and assert both C++ headers use the same override prefix.

./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/planning/iter2-gauge.md:1726:./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/specification/iter3-gauge-prompt.md:7:1. BLOCKING — NFR-1/AC-F4/AC-F5: spec referenced a nonexistent `gen_main` and an unsupported `--repo` flag. The actual CLI is `piketype gen [--namespace NS] <dsl-file>`; the programmatic entry is `piketype.commands.gen.run_gen(path: str, *, namespace: str | None = None)`.
./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/planning/iter2-gauge.md:1727:./specs/002-namespace-cli-argument/artifacts/specification/iter1-gauge.md:7:- **Issue:** The regex accepts C++ keywords and reserved identifiers such as `class`, `namespace`, `__foo`, or `_Bar`. Those can produce invalid or reserved C++ namespaces/include guards despite passing validation. The final namespace also includes `<module_basename>`, but the spec does not require validating that segment.
./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/planning/iter2-gauge.md:1728:./specs/002-namespace-cli-argument/artifacts/specification/iter1-gauge.md:8:- **Suggestion:** Define validation for every generated namespace segment: user-provided segments plus module basename. Reject C++17 keywords and reserved identifier forms, not just regex failures.
./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/planning/iter2-gauge.md:1729:./specs/002-namespace-cli-argument/artifacts/specification/iter1-gauge.md:12:- **Issue:** Applying one prefix to every discovered module and appending only `<module_basename>` means `alpha/typist/types.py` and `beta/typist/types.py` both generate `foo::bar::types`. That silently destroys the path-derived namespace uniqueness the current design relies on.
./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/planning/iter2-gauge.md:1730:./specs/002-namespace-cli-argument/artifacts/specification/iter1-gauge.md:13:- **Suggestion:** Specify collision handling. Either reject duplicate module basenames when `--namespace` is used, or include enough path-derived identity in the composed namespace to keep modules distinct. Add a test for two modules with the same basename.
./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/planning/iter2-gauge.md:1731:./specs/002-namespace-cli-argument/artifacts/specification/iter1-gauge.md:17:- **Issue:** An invalid namespace should exit before generation, so there is no generated output tree to compare against a golden. Calling this a golden-file integration test is wrong.

./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/planning/iter2-gauge.md:1735:./specs/002-namespace-cli-argument/artifacts/specification/iter1-forge.md:8:out). This feature gives users explicit control over the generated C++ namespace

./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/planning/iter2-gauge.md:1740:./specs/002-namespace-cli-argument/artifacts/specification/iter1-forge.md:29:1. **As a hardware/firmware engineer**, I want to specify the C++ namespace for
./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/planning/iter2-gauge.md:1741:./specs/002-namespace-cli-argument/artifacts/specification/iter1-forge.md:31:   namespace hierarchy, without being forced to mirror the DSL file-system layout.
./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/planning/iter2-gauge.md:1742:./specs/002-namespace-cli-argument/artifacts/specification/iter1-forge.md:34:   namespace so that CI scripts can target different namespace schemes per build
./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/planning/iter2-gauge.md:1743:./specs/002-namespace-cli-argument/artifacts/specification/iter1-forge.md:39:### FR-1: New `--namespace` argument on the `gen` subcommand

./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/planning/iter2-gauge.md:1746:./specs/002-namespace-cli-argument/artifacts/specification/iter1-forge.md:46:  (path-derived namespace with `typist` segment filtered).
./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/planning/iter2-gauge.md:1747:./specs/002-namespace-cli-argument/artifacts/specification/iter1-forge.md:58:### FR-3: C++ namespace composition
./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/planning/iter2-gauge.md:1748:./specs/002-namespace-cli-argument/artifacts/specification/iter1-forge.md:60:When `--namespace` is provided, the generated C++ namespace for a module is:
./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/planning/iter2-gauge.md:1749:./specs/002-namespace-cli-argument/artifacts/specification/iter1-forge.md:63:<namespace_value>::<module_basename>

./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/planning/iter2-gauge.md:1751:./specs/002-namespace-cli-argument/artifacts/specification/iter1-forge.md:70:When `--namespace` is **not** provided, the existing path-derived behaviour is
./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/planning/iter2-gauge.md:1752:./specs/002-namespace-cli-argument/artifacts/specification/iter1-forge.md:75:When `--namespace` is provided, the include guard follows the same composition:

./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/planning/iter2-gauge.md:1755:./specs/002-namespace-cli-argument/artifacts/specification/iter1-forge.md:89:SystemVerilog package names, Python module paths, manifest `namespace_parts`, and
./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/planning/iter2-gauge.md:1756:./specs/002-namespace-cli-argument/artifacts/specification/iter1-forge.md:93:are file-path-derived. A C++ namespace override should not silently alter those.
./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/planning/iter2-gauge.md:1757:./specs/002-namespace-cli-argument/artifacts/specification/iter1-forge.md:97:The namespace override value is carried from CLI → `run_gen` → C++ emitter. It

./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/planning/iter2-gauge.md:1809:./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/planning/iter2-forge.md:277:2. `feat(py): module skeleton via templates` (FR-6 sub-step 1) — `module.j2` renders header + imports + footer. `emit_py` constructs `ModuleView` with `body_lines = tuple(legacy_render_body_lines(module))` and the template emits `{{ body_lines | join("\n") }}` for the body. **Byte parity preserved.**
./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/planning/iter2-gauge.md:1810:./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/planning/iter2-forge.md:278:3. `feat(py): top-level type declaration skeletons via templates` (FR-6 sub-step 2) — `_macros.j2` adds `scalar_class_skeleton`, `struct_class_skeleton`, `enum_classes_skeleton`, `flags_class_skeleton` macros that emit *only the class header lines* (`class X:`, `WIDTH = ...`, `BYTE_COUNT = ...`). ./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/planning/iter2-gauge.md:2179:./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/planning/iter1-gauge.md:5356:3. **BLOCKING — FR-6 / FR-10 / NFR-5 / Phase 2.** The C++ plan says the view should precompute “full pack/unpack helper bodies.” That hides meaningful generated structure in Python, directly conflicting with template-first generation. Fix by moving helper skeletons into templates and passing only primitive fields/fragments such as masks, storage types, byte counts, guard, namespace open/close strings, enum defaults, field declarations, and literal spellings.
./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/planning/iter2-gauge.md:2180:./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/planning/iter1-gauge.md:5358:4. **BLOCKING — FR-18 / Phase 2-3 concrete actionability.** C++ and SV view models are not specified enough. “Same shape as Phase 1” does not tell an implementer what fields to build for namespaces, constants, enum wrappers, flags, struct padding, SV localparams, `_pkg`/`_test_pkg`, helper classes, pack/unpack steps, or byte serialization. Fix by adding backend-specific dataclass sketches comparable to the Python sketch.
./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/planning/iter2-gauge.md:2182:./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/planning/iter1-gauge.md:5368:9. **NOTE — C++ namespace override.** The plan mentions namespace/guard precomputation, which is correct, but it should explicitly include the existing `--namespace` override fixture in C++ view/golden validation. This is a known regression point.
./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/planning/iter2-gauge.md:2184:./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/planning/iter1-gauge.md:5380:3. **BLOCKING — FR-6 / FR-10 / NFR-5 / Phase 2.** The C++ plan says the view should precompute “full pack/unpack helper bodies.” That hides meaningful generated structure in Python, directly conflicting with template-first generation. ./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/planning/iter2-gauge.md:2477:   276	1. `feat(py): introduce ModuleView dataclasses and builders` — `view.py` complete, `tests/test_view_py.py` complete. `emitter.py` unchanged. View module is dormant; tests pass against it directly.
./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/planning/iter2-gauge.md:2478:   277	2. `feat(py): module skeleton via templates` (FR-6 sub-step 1) — `module.j2` renders header + imports + footer. `emit_py` constructs `ModuleView` with `body_lines = tuple(legacy_render_body_lines(module))` and the template emits `{{ body_lines | join("\n") }}` for the body. **Byte parity preserved.**
./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/planning/iter2-gauge.md:2479:   278	3. `feat(py): top-level type declaration skeletons via templates` (FR-6 sub-step 2) — `_macros.j2` adds `scalar_class_skeleton`, `struct_class_skeleton`, `enum_classes_skeleton`, `flags_class_skeleton` macros that emit *only the class header lines* (`class X:`, `WIDTH = ...`, `BYTE_COUNT = ...`). Helper-method bodies and expression fragments still arrive as `body_lines_per_type: tuple[str, ...]` strings on each TypeView. The view is augmented with `helper_lines: tuple[str, ...]` per TypeView temporarily. **Byte parity preserved.**
./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/planning/iter2-gauge.md:2480:   279	4. `feat(py): helper-method skeletons via templates` (FR-6 sub-step 3) — macros gain `to_bytes_block`, `from_bytes_block`, `coerce_block`, `setattr_block`, `clone_block`, `eq_repr_block`. Each block consumes precomputed numeric primitives from the view and emits the helper body skeleton. The transitional `helper_lines` field is removed. **Byte parity preserved.**
./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/planning/iter2-gauge.md:2482:   281	6. `refactor(py): remove inline emitter helpers` (FR-19) — delete every `_render_py_*` function and the legacy `render_module_py`. `emit_py` becomes ≤ ~40 lines. **Byte parity preserved.**

./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/planning/iter2-gauge.md:2690:   489	| Performance regression > 25% from Jinja template loading overhead | Low–Medium | Measured at every per-backend completion (FR-25). `jinja2.Environment` caches compiled templates by default within a process. `emit_<lang>` constructs the env once per call, so all modules in a single `gen` invocation share compiled-template caches. Mitigation if exceeded: switch to `jinja2.PackageLoader` with bytecode caching enabled. |

./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/planning/iter2-gauge.md:3341:3. **BLOCKING — FR-6 / FR-10 / NFR-5 / Phase 2.** The C++ plan says the view should precompute “full pack/unpack helper bodies.” That hides meaningful generated structure in Python, directly conflicting with template-first generation. Fix by moving helper skeletons into templates and passing only primitive fields/fragments such as masks, storage types, byte counts, guard, namespace open/close strings, enum defaults, field declarations, and literal spellings.
./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/planning/iter2-gauge.md:3343:4. **BLOCKING — FR-18 / Phase 2-3 concrete actionability.** C++ and SV view models are not specified enough. “Same shape as Phase 1” does not tell an implementer what fields to build for namespaces, constants, enum wrappers, flags, struct padding, SV localparams, `_pkg`/`_test_pkg`, helper classes, pack/unpack steps, or byte serialization. Fix by adding backend-specific dataclass sketches comparable to the Python sketch.
./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/planning/iter2-gauge.md:3353:9. **NOTE — C++ namespace override.** The plan mentions namespace/guard precomputation, which is correct, but it should explicitly include the existing `--namespace` override fixture in C++ view/golden validation. This is a known regression point.

./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/specification/iter1-forge.md:11:The Python, C++, and SystemVerilog backend emitters in `src/piketype/backends/{py,cpp,sv}/emitter.py` are currently single-file inline string-builders. Each file is 700–1100 lines (py: 792, cpp: 1067, sv: 949) and intermixes IR interpretation, formatting decisions, indentation, target-language syntax, helper-method bodies, and special cases. Auditing or extending any backend requires reading long Python functions whose output shape is implicit.
./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/specification/iter1-forge.md:26:- **US-1.** As a maintainer adding a new generated language construct, I want to edit a focused Jinja template alongside a small view-model addition, so that I can change output structure without re-reading 800-line emitter files.
./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/specification/iter1-forge.md:40:- **FR-2.** Each backend SHALL expose one Jinja2 environment scoped to its `templates/` directory using a `FileSystemLoader` rooted at `backends/<lang>/templates/`. Environment options SHALL include `keep_trailing_newline=True`, `trim_blocks=True`, `lstrip_blocks=True`, `undefined=jinja2.StrictUndefined`, and `autoescape=False`. Environments SHALL be constructed once per `emit_<lang>` call (not module-global) to keep emitter state local and testable.
./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/specification/iter1-forge.md:43:  - A helper to render a template and return a `str` whose final character is `"\n"` (consistent with current emitter contracts).
./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/specification/iter1-forge.md:44:- **FR-4.** Each backend SHALL keep its existing public emit entry point (`emit_py`, `emit_cpp`, `emit_sv`) with unchanged signatures and unchanged write locations; only the internal rendering pipeline changes. The IR → view-model → template → string flow SHALL be the only public migration surface.
./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/specification/iter1-forge.md:48:- **FR-5.** Migration SHALL proceed in this order: (1) Python backend, (2) C++ backend, (3) SystemVerilog backend. Each backend migration SHALL be a separable, independently reviewable change. Subsequent backends SHALL NOT begin until the prior backend's golden tests pass byte-for-byte and its inline emitter has been removed.
./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/specification/iter1-forge.md:95:- **FR-19.** When a backend's migration is complete, the inline `render_module_<lang>` function and its helpers in `backends/<lang>/emitter.py` SHALL be removed (no dead code retained "just in case"). The `emit_<lang>` entry point remains.
./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/specification/iter1-forge.md:117:- **NFR-7. Stability of public API.** The `emit_py`, `emit_cpp`, `emit_sv` function signatures and import paths SHALL NOT change. CLI behavior SHALL NOT change.
./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/specification/iter1-forge.md:124:- **AC-2.** `find src/piketype/backends/{py,cpp,sv}/templates -name '*.j2'` lists at least one template per backend, and each backend's `emit_<lang>` calls into a Jinja2 `Environment` to produce its output.
./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/specification/iter1-forge.md:125:- **AC-3.** Each backend's `emitter.py` has shrunk relative to its pre-migration line count. Helper functions whose responsibility is now in templates have been removed (no dead code).
./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/specification/iter1-forge.md:138:- **OOS-2.** Migrating the runtime, build, lint, or test sub-backends under `backends/{runtime,build,lint,test}/`. Those are out of scope for v1 of this feature; only the three primary code emitters (py, cpp, sv) are in scope.
./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/specification/iter1-forge.md:153:- **[NEEDS CLARIFICATION Q-5]** How should the migration treat `backends/runtime/emitter.py` (the runtime support package generator)? It is currently the only emitter that already has imports of "Template" related symbols (per the earlier grep). Is it in scope as a fourth migration, deferred to a follow-up spec, or considered out of scope per OOS-2? **Recommendation:** explicitly out of scope here (per OOS-2); follow-up spec after the three primary backends land. Confirm.
./specs/004-cpp-constexpr-upper-case/artifacts/specification/iter1-forge.md:10:- **US-2:** As a typist maintainer, I want the emitter code to produce `UPPER_SNAKE_CASE` constant names, so that golden tests reflect the canonical style and future regressions are caught.
./specs/004-cpp-constexpr-upper-case/artifacts/specification/iter1-forge.md:70:- **AC-1:** The C++ emitter (`src/typist/backends/cpp/emitter.py`) produces `UPPER_SNAKE_CASE` for all `constexpr` names listed in FR-1, FR-2, and FR-3.
./specs/004-cpp-constexpr-upper-case/artifacts/specification/iter1-forge.md:71:- **AC-2:** The runtime emitter (`src/typist/backends/runtime/emitter.py`) produces `VERBOSE_DEFAULT` instead of `kVerboseDefault`.
./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/planning/iter3-gauge.md:25:2. BLOCKING — C++ byte-parity issues in `CppNamespaceView` (nested `piketype`) and `standard_includes` (alphabetization claim).
./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/planning/iter3-gauge.md:39:2. Confirm `CppNamespaceView` matches current emitter byte output (single C++17 nested namespace line, `piketype` filtered, `has_namespace` bool guards open/close lines).
./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/planning/iter3-gauge.md:40:3. Confirm `standard_includes` is declaration-order (cstdint always; cstddef/stdexcept/vector if has_types) — NOT alphabetized.
./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/planning/iter3-gauge.md:51:The prompt is a planning-stage gauge review. I’m reading the iter3 plan and the prior gauge output, then I’ll verify the specific regression points against the current code where byte-parity claims depend on emitters.
./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/specification/iter3-gauge-prompt.md:7:1. BLOCKING — NFR-1/AC-F4/AC-F5: spec referenced a nonexistent `gen_main` and an unsupported `--repo` flag. The actual CLI is `piketype gen [--namespace NS] <dsl-file>`; the programmatic entry is `piketype.commands.gen.run_gen(path: str, *, namespace: str | None = None)`.
./specs/008-enum-dsl-type/artifacts/specification/iter3-gauge.md:3:**BLOCKING**: C++20 defaulted equality still mandated while constitution targets C++17. Existing pattern claim is factually true (scalar and struct wrappers already emit it), but does not resolve the spec/constitution conflict. Either change the project target to C++20 or require a manual C++17 equality operator.
./specs/008-enum-dsl-type/artifacts/specification/iter3-gauge.md:5:**WARNING**: C++ enum values above `INT64_MAX` may use `static_cast<uint_type>(value)`, but that is not enough if the value is emitted as an unsuffixed decimal literal (ill-formed before the cast). Require unsigned literal spelling via `_cpp_unsigned_literal()`.

./specs/002-namespace-cli-argument/artifacts/planning/plan.md:41:2. **`tests/test_namespace_validation.py`** — Unit tests for validation logic
./specs/002-namespace-cli-argument/artifacts/planning/plan.md:50:3. **`tests/fixtures/namespace_override/project/`** — Multi-module fixture
./specs/002-namespace-cli-argument/artifacts/planning/plan.md:52:   - `beta/typist/more.py` — More constants (different path-derived namespace).
./specs/002-namespace-cli-argument/artifacts/planning/plan.md:55:4. **`tests/goldens/gen/namespace_override/`** — Golden output for the fixture
./specs/002-namespace-cli-argument/artifacts/planning/plan.md:64:     baseline (SV/Python/manifest unaffected by `--namespace`).
./specs/002-namespace-cli-argument/artifacts/planning/plan.md:70:5. **`src/typist/cli.py`** — Add `--namespace` to `gen` subparser
./specs/002-namespace-cli-argument/artifacts/planning/plan.md:71:   - Break out `gen` from the shared loop to add `--namespace` as an optional arg.
./specs/002-namespace-cli-argument/artifacts/planning/plan.md:72:   - Pass `args.namespace` (which is `None` when omitted) to `run_gen`.
./specs/002-namespace-cli-argument/artifacts/planning/plan.md:74:6. **`src/typist/commands/gen.py`** — Accept and thread `namespace` parameter
./specs/002-namespace-cli-argument/artifacts/planning/plan.md:75:   - `run_gen(path: str, *, namespace: str | None = None)` — New kwarg.
./specs/002-namespace-cli-argument/artifacts/planning/plan.md:76:   - Call `validate_cpp_namespace()` before discovery if `namespace` is not None.
./specs/002-namespace-cli-argument/artifacts/planning/plan.md:77:   - After discovery, if `namespace` is set, call `check_duplicate_basenames()`.
./specs/002-namespace-cli-argument/artifacts/planning/plan.md:78:   - Pass `namespace` to `emit_cpp()`.
./specs/002-namespace-cli-argument/artifacts/planning/plan.md:80:7. **`src/typist/backends/cpp/emitter.py`** — Use namespace override
./specs/002-namespace-cli-argument/artifacts/planning/plan.md:81:   - `emit_cpp(repo, *, namespace: str | None = None)` — New kwarg.
./specs/002-namespace-cli-argument/artifacts/planning/plan.md:82:   - `render_module_hpp(module, *, namespace: str | None = None)` — New kwarg.
./specs/002-namespace-cli-argument/artifacts/planning/plan.md:83:   - When `namespace` is not None:

./specs/008-enum-dsl-type/artifacts/specification/iter4-forge.md:137:- `enum class <base>_enum_t : <uint_type> { <NAME0> = <val0>, ... };` where `<uint_type>` is the smallest unsigned integer type that fits `resolved_width` (using the existing `_cpp_scalar_value_type()` helper with `signed=False`). Enum initializer values must use the existing `_cpp_unsigned_literal()` helper to emit properly-suffixed unsigned literals (e.g., `42U`, `12345ULL`), avoiding ill-formed unsuffixed decimal literals for values exceeding `INT64_MAX`.

./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/planning/iter1-gauge.md:273:The Python, C++, and SystemVerilog backend emitters in `src/piketype/backends/{py,cpp,sv}/emitter.py` are currently single-file inline string-builders. Each file is 700–1100 lines (py: 792, cpp: 1067, sv: 949) and intermixes IR interpretation, formatting decisions, indentation, target-language syntax, helper-method bodies, and special cases. Auditing or extending any backend requires reading long Python functions whose output shape is implicit.
./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/planning/iter1-gauge.md:288:- **US-1.** As a maintainer adding a new generated language construct, I want to edit a focused Jinja template alongside a small view-model addition, so that I can change output structure without re-reading 800-line emitter files.
./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/planning/iter1-gauge.md:312:- **FR-2.** Each backend SHALL expose one Jinja2 `Environment` constructed via `jinja2.PackageLoader("piketype.backends.<lang>", "templates")`. Environment options SHALL be exactly: `keep_trailing_newline=True`, `trim_blocks=True`, `lstrip_blocks=True`, `undefined=jinja2.StrictUndefined`, `autoescape=False`. The environment SHALL be constructed once per `emit_<lang>` call (not module-global) so that emitter state is local and testable. `FileSystemLoader` SHALL NOT be used for production rendering. (Tests MAY construct ad-hoc environments with `DictLoader` or `FileSystemLoader` for unit testing of templates.)
./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/planning/iter1-gauge.md:315:  - `def render(*, env: jinja2.Environment, template_name: str, context: object) -> str` that renders a template and returns a string whose final character is `"\n"` (consistent with current emitter contracts). The `context` SHALL be a view-model dataclass instance; the helper SHALL pass its fields as the template context (via `dataclasses.asdict` or by passing the instance directly with templates accessing `.field` attributes — implementation choice, but uniform across backends).
./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/planning/iter1-gauge.md:316:- **FR-4.** Each backend SHALL keep its existing public emit entry point (`emit_py`, `emit_cpp`, `emit_sv`) with unchanged signatures and unchanged write locations; only the internal rendering pipeline changes. The IR → view-model → template → string flow SHALL be the only public migration surface.
./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/planning/iter1-gauge.md:329:  **Examples of "trivial fragments" (MAY remain inline in Python and be passed as `str` fields):** a single hex literal (`"0xff"`), a numeric expression rendered for a constant (`"(1 << 32) - 1"`), a single-line type alias initializer, a header comment line, an `__all__` tuple body. These fragments SHALL be pre-computed in Python and exposed as `str` fields on the view model; templates SHALL emit them with `{{ fragment }}` and SHALL NOT recompute them.
./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/planning/iter1-gauge.md:365:- **FR-13.** Template file extensions SHALL be `.j2`. Templates SHALL NOT carry the machine-readable header themselves; the header is produced by `backends/common/headers.py` and passed in as a view-model `header: str` field, then emitted by the template with `{{ header }}`.
./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/planning/iter1-gauge.md:385:- **FR-19.** When a backend's migration is complete, the inline `render_module_<lang>` function and its `_render_*` helpers in `backends/<lang>/emitter.py` SHALL be removed. The `emit_<lang>` entry point remains and now orchestrates: (1) build view model from IR, (2) construct env, (3) render primary template, (4) write file.
./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/planning/iter1-gauge.md:416:- **NFR-1. Performance.** The wall-clock time of `piketype gen` against fixture `tests/fixtures/struct_padded` SHALL NOT regress by more than 25% relative to the pre-migration baseline. The actual CLI signature is `piketype gen [--namespace NS] <dsl-file-path>`, where `<dsl-file-path>` is a single Python DSL file (e.g., `tests/fixtures/struct_padded/project/alpha/piketype/types.py`) — not a project root.
./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/planning/iter1-gauge.md:434:- **NFR-7. Stability of public API.** The `emit_py`, `emit_cpp`, `emit_sv` function signatures and import paths SHALL NOT change. CLI behavior SHALL NOT change.
./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/planning/iter1-gauge.md:447:- **AC-2-`<lang>`.** `find src/piketype/backends/<lang>/templates -name '*.j2' | wc -l` is at least 1 and `backends/<lang>/emitter.py` calls `render` from `backends/common/render.py` (verifiable by `grep -n "from piketype.backends.common.render import" src/piketype/backends/<lang>/emitter.py`).
./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/planning/iter1-gauge.md:448:- **AC-3-`<lang>`.** `backends/<lang>/emitter.py` line count has decreased relative to its pre-migration count (commit `develop`@HEAD-at-spec-creation). Helpers `_render_*` whose responsibility moved to a template are removed (no dead code).
./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/planning/iter1-gauge.md:472:- **OOS-2.** Migrating the runtime, build, lint, or test sub-backends under `backends/{runtime,build,lint,test}/`. Those are out of scope for this feature; only the three primary code emitters (py, cpp, sv) are in scope. Follow-up specs may address them.
./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/planning/iter1-gauge.md:485:- **CL-1 (was Q-1) — Per-backend commit granularity.** Each backend's migration lands as **multiple contiguous commits** on `feature/010-jinja-template-migration`, one per FR-6 sub-step where the change is non-trivial (skeleton, top-level types, helpers, fragments). The first commit of a backend's migration block introduces `view.py` and the primary template; subsequent commits replace inline emitter sections with template-rendered output. Squash-on-merge is permitted by project policy but SHALL NOT be required by this spec. Reverting any backend's migration is a contiguous-range revert per NFR-6.

./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/planning/iter1-gauge.md:4282:   103	- **FR-13.** Template file extensions SHALL be `.j2`. Templates SHALL NOT carry the machine-readable header themselves; the header is produced by `backends/common/headers.py` and passed in as a view-model `header: str` field, then emitted by the template with `{{ header }}`.
./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/planning/iter1-gauge.md:4302:   123	- **FR-19.** When a backend's migration is complete, the inline `render_module_<lang>` function and its `_render_*` helpers in `backends/<lang>/emitter.py` SHALL be removed. The `emit_<lang>` entry point remains and now orchestrates: (1) build view model from IR, (2) construct env, (3) render primary template, (4) write file.
./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/planning/iter1-gauge.md:4333:   154	- **NFR-1. Performance.** The wall-clock time of `piketype gen` against fixture `tests/fixtures/struct_padded` SHALL NOT regress by more than 25% relative to the pre-migration baseline. The actual CLI signature is `piketype gen [--namespace NS] <dsl-file-path>`, where `<dsl-file-path>` is a single Python DSL file (e.g., `tests/fixtures/struct_padded/project/alpha/piketype/types.py`) — not a project root.
./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/planning/iter1-gauge.md:4351:   172	- **NFR-7. Stability of public API.** The `emit_py`, `emit_cpp`, `emit_sv` function signatures and import paths SHALL NOT change. CLI behavior SHALL NOT change.
./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/planning/iter1-gauge.md:4364:   185	- **AC-2-`<lang>`.** `find src/piketype/backends/<lang>/templates -name '*.j2' | wc -l` is at least 1 and `backends/<lang>/emitter.py` calls `render` from `backends/common/render.py` (verifiable by `grep -n "from piketype.backends.common.render import" src/piketype/backends/<lang>/emitter.py`).
./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/planning/iter1-gauge.md:4365:   186	- **AC-3-`<lang>`.** `backends/<lang>/emitter.py` line count has decreased relative to its pre-migration count (commit `develop`@HEAD-at-spec-creation). Helpers `_render_*` whose responsibility moved to a template are removed (no dead code).
./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/planning/iter1-gauge.md:4389:   210	- **OOS-2.** Migrating the runtime, build, lint, or test sub-backends under `backends/{runtime,build,lint,test}/`. Those are out of scope for this feature; only the three primary code emitters (py, cpp, sv) are in scope. Follow-up specs may address them.
./specs/010-jinja-template-migration/artifacts/planning/iter1-gauge.md:4402:   223	- **CL-1 (was Q-1) — Per-backend commit granularity.** Each backend's migration lands as **multiple contiguous commits** on `feature/010-jinja-template-migration`, one per FR-6 sub-step where the change is non-trivial (skeleton, top-level types, helpers, fragments). The first commit of a backend's migration block introduces `view.py` and the primary template; subsequent commits replace inline emitter sections with template-rendered output. Squash-on-merge is permitted by project policy but SHALL NOT be required by this spec. Reverting any backend's migration is a contiguous-range revert per NFR-6.
